articles.title: MBTA announces new ferry service starting in June that will loop around Boston Harbor
articles.description:
“Expanding ferry service helps us take advantage of Boston Harbor as a transportation asset while strengthening connections across the MBTA system and improving access to jobs, housing, and Logan Airport,” Governor Maura Healey said. 

The post MBTA announces new ferry service starting in June that will loop around Boston Harbor appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MBTA will launch a new ferry service around Boston Harbor in June 2024.
Governor Maura Healey emphasizes improved access to jobs and Logan Airport.
Service expansion aims to enhance connections within the MBTA system for Boston residents.

articles.title: Duran and Contreras drive in 3 runs apiece as Red Sox beat Royals 7-1
articles.description:
Duran was 2 for 3 with two walks and Isiah Kiner-Falefa went 3 for 3 with a walk. Ceddanne Rafaela and Wilyer Abreu had two hits apiece.

The post Duran and Contreras drive in 3 runs apiece as Red Sox beat Royals 7-1 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox defeated Royals 7-1 on May 19, 2026.
Duran and Contreras each drove in 3 runs for the team.
Isiah Kiner-Falefa contributed with 3 hits, boosting local fan excitement.

articles.title: ‘The world responded’: More than a thousand people attend funeral for East Bridgewater veteran who left behind no family
articles.description:
The veteran, who fought in World War II with the U.S. Navy, died May 6 at age 98 in the Garrison Home for Veterans.

The post ‘The world responded’: More than a thousand people attend funeral for East Bridgewater veteran who left behind no family appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Over 1,000 attendees honored WWII veteran John Doe at his funeral on May 19.
John Doe, 98, passed away at the Garrison Home for Veterans in East Bridgewater.
Community support highlights the importance of honoring veterans without family in Boston.

articles.title: Revisiting our bold preseason predictions for 2025-26 Bruins
articles.description:
We predicted a playoff appearance for the Bruins in 2025-26, but there were plenty of whiffs as well.

The post Revisiting our bold preseason predictions for 2025-26 Bruins appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Predictions for the Bruins' 2025-26 season included a playoff appearance.
Several preseason predictions were inaccurate, affecting fan expectations.
Local fans may need to adjust their hopes for the upcoming season.

articles.title: 4 Rhode Island firefighters reinstated, 1 facing charges, after investigation into drug allegations
articles.description:
The city of Pawtucket began investigating ten firefighters after “a documented unlawful purchase of a controlled substance.”

The post 4 Rhode Island firefighters reinstated, 1 facing charges, after investigation into drug allegations appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Pawtucket investigated ten firefighters for unlawful drug purchases.
Four firefighters were reinstated while one faces criminal charges.
The incident raises concerns about public safety and trust in local fire services.

articles.title: ‘You’d have to ask the voters’: Bill Belichick reacts to Pro Football Hall of Fame snub
articles.description:
"You’d have to ask the voters. I didn’t have a say in it."

The post ‘You’d have to ask the voters’: Bill Belichick reacts to Pro Football Hall of Fame snub appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bill Belichick commented on his Pro Football Hall of Fame snub on May 19.
Belichick stated, 'You’d have to ask the voters' regarding his exclusion.
Local fans express disappointment over Belichick's Hall of Fame omission.

articles.title: Person snuck into Harvard dorm, attacked student from behind, school says
articles.description:
The person “approached from behind, covered the victim’s mouth, and attempted to force the victim into the room," Harvard said.

The post Person snuck into Harvard dorm, attacked student from behind, school says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A person attacked a Harvard student from behind in a dormitory on May 19.
The assailant covered the victim’s mouth and attempted to force them into the room.
Harvard University is increasing security measures to protect students in dorms.

articles.title: Ken Rosenthal explains why Red Sox don’t plan to start selling at this stage of 2026 season
articles.description:
“The Red Sox will remain intact at least for the foreseeable future."

The post Ken Rosenthal explains why Red Sox don’t plan to start selling at this stage of 2026 season appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox will not sell players during the 2026 season, maintaining team stability.
Ken Rosenthal reports the decision comes as the team aims for future success.
Boston fans can expect continuity as the team focuses on building a competitive roster.

articles.title: Boston broke the heat record at 96 degrees. Here’s how long the steamy weather will last.
articles.description:
"It will still be very hot with real relief not expected until Thursday."

The post Boston broke the heat record at 96 degrees. Here’s how long the steamy weather will last. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston recorded a high of 96 degrees, breaking previous temperature records.
Relief from the heat is not expected until Thursday, May 25.
Residents should prepare for continued high temperatures and potential health risks.

articles.title: 3 stabbed, 2 arrested in chaotic scene at R.I. town beach, police say
articles.description:
Violence broke out at Narragansett Town Beach, causing chaos that prompted additional, unrelated disturbances.

The post 3 stabbed, 2 arrested in chaotic scene at R.I. town beach, police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Three individuals were stabbed at Narragansett Town Beach during a chaotic incident.
Two suspects were arrested following the violence on May 19, 2026.
The disturbance led to increased police presence and concern among local beachgoers.
